page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i3.xlarge is a memory-optimized and storage-oriented virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance includes a bundled 959 GB local SSD for high-speed storage access and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

In terms of performance, the Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AVX-512 VNNI, which assist with vector and neural network calculations. However, benchmark results indicate that the instance generally falls into the lower performance tiers for raw compute. Both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU benchmarks place the instance in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Similarly, Geekbench multi-core scores and Passmark CPU scores reside in the weak category, though Geekbench single-core performance and Passmark memory scores reach average levels.

The storage and memory architecture of the ecs.i3.xlarge presents a clear tradeoff. While it offers a substantial 959 GB of local SSD storage, memory bandwidth benchmarks for both small and large blocks are relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests, also perform in the lower 25% tier. This suggests that while the instance provides high local storage capacity and a generous memory allocation, it is not optimized for high-throughput, memory-bound transactional workloads.

Given these characteristics, the Alibaba Cloud ecs.i3.xlarge is best suited for workloads that require a balance of high local storage capacity and moderate memory capacity without demanding extreme CPU throughput. Suitable use cases include caching layers, moderate database hosting, and general-purpose workloads that benefit from local SSD storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i3.xlarge is a memory-optimized and storage-focused virtual machine instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor clocked at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, and includes a bundled 959 GB local SSD alongside a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. While the CPU supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512, benchmark data indicates that raw compute and memory bandwidth performance generally fall into the lower 25% tier, though Geekbench single-core and Passmark memory scores achieve average rankings. This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring significant local storage and memory capacity without demanding high-intensity CPU throughput. It is well-suited for moderate database hosting, caching layers, and storage-heavy general-purpose applications.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i3.xlarge features 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a bundled 959 GB local SSD. It is designed for storage-heavy and memory-intensive workloads requiring moderate compute.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i3.xlarge is a KVM-based instance with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a bundled 959 GB local SSD for storage-focused workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a bundled 959 GB local SSD and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for storage-heavy workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Bundled 959 GB local SSD storage for high-capacity local data access
Supports AVX-512 and AVX-512 VNNI instruction sets
Best suited for moderate databases, caching layers, and storage-heavy applications
